objective: Add a small GET /readyz endpoint to the FastAPI in-memory task-board API. The endpoint must return exactly {"ready": true}. Add pytest coverage and keep all existing tests passing. This scenario validates that the restarted ORIS bridge no longer leaves tracked task-run evidence dirty after committing ORIS evidence.
constraints: Do not ask the human for routine engineering decisions.
Use the existing FastAPI app in app/main.py.
Keep the implementation minimal and deterministic.
Do not change existing /health, /healthz, /info, /version, /stats, or /tasks response semantics except if needed to keep tests passing.
Do not commit .env, credentials, private keys, .venv, caches, browser profiles, or runtime queue JSON.
Produce strict autonomous result schema and fresh skill resolver evidence.
final_checks:
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from app.main import app
client = TestClient(app)
r = client.get('/readyz')
assert r.status_code == 200, r.text
assert r.json() == {'ready': True}, r.json()
notes: Before running this scenario, restart oris-dev-employee-bridge.service so the bridge process loads the post-commit clean fix.
Harness report final_oris_tracked_status should be empty before the report commit step.
